     2	// An SMTP Transaction
     3	
     4	const util = require('node:util')
     5	
     6	const Notes = require('haraka-notes')
     7	const utils = require('haraka-utils')
     8	const message = require('haraka-email-message')
     9	
    10	class Transaction {
    11	    constructor(uuid, cfg = {}) {
    12	        this.uuid = uuid || utils.uuid()
    13	        this.cfg = cfg
    14	        this.mail_from = null
    15	        this.rcpt_to = []
    16	        this.header_lines = []
    17	        this.data_lines = []
    18	        this.attachment_start_hooks = []
    19	        this.banner = null
    20	        this.body_filters = []
    21	        this.data_bytes = 0
    22	        this.header_pos = 0
    23	        this.found_hb_sep = false
    24	        this.body = null
    25	        this.parse_body = false
    26	        this.notes = new Notes()
    27	        this.notes.skip_plugins = []
    28	        this.header = new message.Header()
    29	        this.message_stream = new message.stream(this.cfg, this.uuid, this.header.header_list)
    30	        this.discard_data = false
    31	        this.resetting = false
    32	        this.rcpt_count = {
    33	            accept: 0,
    34	            tempfail: 0,
    35	            reject: 0,
    36	        }
    37	        this.msg_status = undefined
    38	        this.data_post_start = null
    39	        this.data_post_delay = 0
    40	        this.encoding = 'utf8'
    41	        this.mime_part_count = 0
    42	    }
    43	
    44	    ensure_body() {
    45	        if (this.body) return
    46	
    47	        this.body = new message.Body(this.header)
    48	        this.body.on('mime_boundary', (m) => this.incr_mime_count())
    49	
    50	        for (const hook of this.attachment_start_hooks) {
    51	            this.body.on('attachment_start', hook)
    52	        }
    53	
    54	        if (this.banner) this.body.set_banner(this.banner)
    55	
    56	        for (const o of this.body_filters) {
    57	            this.body.add_filter((ct, enc, buf) => {
    58	                const re_match = util.types.isRegExp(o.ct_match) && o.ct_match.test(ct.toLowerCase())
    59	                const ct_begins = ct.toLowerCase().indexOf(String(o.ct_match).toLowerCase()) === 0
    60	                if (re_match || ct_begins) return o.filter(ct, enc, buf)
    61	            })
    62	        }
    63	    }
    64	
    65	    // Removes the CR of a CRLF newline at the end of the buffer.
    66	    remove_final_cr(data) {
    67	        if (data.length < 2) return data
    68	        if (!Buffer.isBuffer(data)) data = Buffer.from(data)
    69	
    70	        if (data[data.length - 2] === 0x0d && data[data.length - 1] === 0x0a) {
    71	            data[data.length - 2] = 0x0a
    72	            return data.slice(0, data.length - 1)
    73	        }
    74	        return data
    75	    }
    76	
    77	    // Duplicates any '.' chars at the beginning of a line (dot-stuffing) and
    78	    // ensures all newlines are CRLF.
    79	    add_dot_stuffing_and_ensure_crlf_newlines(data) {
    80	        if (!data.length) return data
    81	        if (!Buffer.isBuffer(data)) data = Buffer.from(data)
    82	
    83	        // Make a new buffer big enough to hold two bytes for every one input
    84	        // byte.  At most, we add one extra character per input byte, so this
    85	        // is always big enough.  We allocate it "unsafe" (i.e. no memset) for
    86	        // speed because we're about to fill it with data, and the remainder of
    87	        // the space we don't fill will be sliced away before we return this.
    88	        const output = Buffer.allocUnsafe(data.length * 2)
    89	        let output_pos = 0
    90	
    91	        let input_pos = 0
    92	        let next_dot = data.indexOf(0x2e)
    93	        let next_lf = data.indexOf(0x0a)
    94	        while (next_dot !== -1 || next_lf !== -1) {
    95	            const run_end = next_dot !== -1 && (next_lf === -1 || next_dot < next_lf) ? next_dot : next_lf
    96	
    97	            // Copy up to whichever comes first, '.' or '\n' (but don't
    98	            // copy the '.' or '\n' itself).
    99	            data.copy(output, output_pos, input_pos, run_end)
   100	            output_pos += run_end - input_pos
   101	
   102	            if (data[run_end] === 0x2e && (run_end === 0 || data[run_end - 1] === 0x0a)) {
   103	                // Replace /^\./ with '..'
   104	                output[output_pos++] = 0x2e
   105	            } else if (data[run_end] === 0x0a && (run_end === 0 || data[run_end - 1] !== 0x0d)) {
   106	                // Replace /\r?\n/ with '\r\n'
   107	                output[output_pos++] = 0x0d
   108	            }
   109	            output[output_pos++] = data[run_end]
   110	
   111	            input_pos = run_end + 1
   112	
   113	            if (run_end === next_dot) {
   114	                next_dot = data.indexOf(0x2e, input_pos)
   115	            } else {
   116	                next_lf = data.indexOf(0x0a, input_pos)
   117	            }
   118	        }
   119	
   120	        if (input_pos < data.length) {
   121	            data.copy(output, output_pos, input_pos)
   122	            output_pos += data.length - input_pos
   123	        }
   124	
   125	        return output.slice(0, output_pos)
   126	    }
   127	
   128	    add_data(line) {
   129	        if (typeof line === 'string') {
   130	            // This shouldn't ever happen.
   131	            line = Buffer.from(line, this.encoding)
   132	        }
   133	        // is this the end of headers line?
   134	        if (this.header_pos === 0 && (line[0] === 0x0a || (line[0] === 0x0d && line[1] === 0x0a))) {
   135	            this.header.parse(this.header_lines)
   136	            this.header_pos = this.header_lines.length
   137	            this.found_hb_sep = true
   138	            if (this.parse_body) this.ensure_body()
   139	        } else if (this.header_pos === 0) {
   140	            // Build up headers
   141	            if (this.header_lines.length < (this.cfg?.headers?.max_lines || 1000)) {
   142	                if (line[0] === 0x2e) line = line.slice(1) // Strip leading '.'
   143	                this.header_lines.push(line.toString(this.encoding).replace(/\r\n$/, '\n'))
   144	            }
   145	        } else if (this.parse_body) {
   146	            let new_line = line
   147	            if (new_line[0] === 0x2e) new_line = new_line.slice(1) // Strip leading "."
   148	
   149	            line = this.add_dot_stuffing_and_ensure_crlf_newlines(this.body.parse_more(this.remove_final_cr(new_line)))
   150	
   151	            if (!line.length) return // buffering for banners
   152	        }
   153	
   154	        if (!this.discard_data) this.message_stream.add_line(line)
   155	    }
   156	
   157	    end_data(cb) {
   158	        if (!this.found_hb_sep && this.header_lines.length) {
   159	            // Headers not parsed yet - must be a busted email
   160	            // Strategy: Find the first line that doesn't look like a header.
   161	            // Treat anything before that as headers, anything after as body.
   162	            let header_pos = 0
   163	            for (let i = 0; i < this.header_lines.length; i++) {
   164	                // Anything that doesn't match a header or continuation
   165	                if (!/^(?:([^\s:]*):\s*([\s\S]*)$|[ \t])/.test(this.header_lines[i])) {
   166	                    break
   167	                }
   168	                header_pos = i
   169	            }
   170	            const body_lines = this.header_lines.splice(header_pos + 1)
   171	            this.header.parse(this.header_lines)
   172	            this.header_pos = header_pos
   173	            if (this.parse_body) {
   174	                this.ensure_body()
   175	                for (const bodyLine of body_lines) {
   176	                    this.body.parse_more(bodyLine)
   177	                }
   178	            }
   179	        }
   180	        if (this.header_pos && this.parse_body) {
   181	            const line = this.add_dot_stuffing_and_ensure_crlf_newlines(this.body.parse_end())
   182	            if (line.length) {
   183	                this.body.force_end()
   184	
   185	                if (!this.discard_data) this.message_stream.add_line(line)
   186	            }
   187	        }
   188	
   189	        if (this.discard_data) {
   190	            cb()
   191	        } else {
   192	            this.message_stream.add_line_end(cb)
   193	        }
   194	    }
   195	
   196	    add_header(key, value) {
   197	        this.header.add_end(key, value)
   198	        if (this.header_pos > 0) this.reset_headers()
   199	    }
   200	
   201	    add_leading_header(key, value) {
   202	        this.header.add(key, value)
   203	        if (this.header_pos > 0) this.reset_headers()
   204	    }
   205	
   206	    reset_headers() {
   207	        const header_lines = this.header.lines()
   208	        this.header_pos = header_lines.length
   209	    }
   210	
   211	    remove_header(key) {
   212	        this.header.remove(key)
   213	        if (this.header_pos > 0) this.reset_headers()
   214	    }
   215	
   216	    attachment_hooks(start, data, end) {
   217	        this.parse_body = true
   218	        this.attachment_start_hooks.push(start)
   219	    }
   220	
   221	    set_banner(text, html) {
   222	        // throw "transaction.set_banner is currently non-functional";
   223	        this.parse_body = true
   224	        if (!html) {
   225	            html = text.replace(/\n/g, '<br/>\n')
   226	        }
   227	        this.banner = [text, html]
   228	    }
   229	
   230	    add_body_filter(ct_match, filter) {
   231	        this.parse_body = true
   232	        this.body_filters.push({ ct_match, filter })
   233	    }
   234	
   235	    incr_mime_count(line) {
   236	        this.mime_part_count++
   237	    }
   238	}
   239	
   240	exports.Transaction = Transaction
   241	
   242	exports.createTransaction = (uuid, cfg) => {
   243	    return new Transaction(uuid, cfg)
   244	}
   245

     1	'use strict'
     2	
     3	const libmime = require('libmime')
     4	const libqp = require('libqp')
     5	const logger = require('./logger')
     6	const { try_convert } = require('./encoding')
     7	
     8	/* eslint no-control-regex: 0 */
     9	
    10	function _decode_header(matched, encoding, lang, cte, data) {
    11	  cte = cte.toUpperCase()
    12	  switch (cte) {
    13	    case 'Q':
    14	      data = libqp.decode(data.replace(/_/g, ' '))
    15	      break
    16	    case 'B':
    17	      data = Buffer.from(data, 'base64')
    18	      break
    19	    default:
    20	      logger.logerror(`Invalid header encoding type: ${cte}`)
    21	  }
    22	  if (!/UTF-?8/i.test(encoding)) return try_convert(data, encoding)
    23	  return data.toString()
    24	}
    25	
    26	function _parse_rfc2231(params, str) {
    27	  while (true) {
    28	    const sub_matches =
    29	      /(([!#$%&'*+.0-9A-Zdiff^_`a-z{|}~-]*)\*)(\d*)=(\s*".*?[^\\]";?|\S*)/.exec(
    30	        str,
    31	      )
    32	    if (!sub_matches) return
    33	
    34	    const key = sub_matches[1]
    35	    let key_actual = sub_matches[2]
    36	    let key_id = sub_matches[3] || '0'
    37	    let value = sub_matches[4].replace(/;$/, '')
    38	
    39	    str = str.replace(sub_matches[0], '')
    40	
    41	    const key_extract = /^(.*?)(\*(\d+)\*)$/.exec(key)
    42	    if (key_extract) {
    43	      key_actual = key_extract[1]
    44	      key_id = key_extract[3]
    45	    }
    46	
    47	    const quote = /^\s*"(.*)"$/.exec(value)
    48	    if (quote) value = quote[1]
    49	
    50	    const lang_match = /^(.*?)'(.*?)'(.*)/.exec(value)
    51	    if (lang_match) {
    52	      if (key_actual === params.cur_key && lang_match[2] !== params.cur_lang) {
    53	        continue
    54	      }
    55	      params.cur_enc = lang_match[1]
    56	      params.cur_lang = lang_match[2]
    57	      value = lang_match[3]
    58	    } else if (key_actual !== params.cur_key) {
    59	      params.cur_lang = ''
    60	      params.cur_enc = ''
    61	    }
    62	
    63	    params.cur_key = key_actual
    64	    params.keys[key_actual] = ''
    65	    params.kv[`${key_actual}*${key_id}`] = value
    66	  }
    67	}
    68	
    69	function _decode_rfc2231(params, str) {
    70	  _parse_rfc2231(params, str)
    71	
    72	  for (const key in params.keys) {
    73	    str += ` ${key}="`
    74	    /* eslint no-constant-condition: 0 */
    75	    let merged = ''
    76	    for (let i = 0; true; i++) {
    77	      const _val = params.kv[`${key}*${i}`]
    78	      if (_val === undefined) break
    79	      merged += _val
    80	    }
    81	
    82	    try {
    83	      merged = decodeURIComponent(merged)
    84	    } catch {
    85	      logger.logerror(`Decode header failed: ${key}: ${merged}`)
    86	    }
    87	
    88	    if (params.cur_enc) {
    89	      merged = try_convert(Buffer.from(merged, 'utf8'), params.cur_enc)
    90	    }
    91	
    92	    str += `${merged}";`
    93	  }
    94	
    95	  return str
    96	}
    97	
    98	// An RFC 2822 email header parser
    99	class Header {
   100	  #options
   101	
   102	  constructor(options) {
   103	    this.headers = Object.create(null)
   104	    this.headers_decoded = Object.create(null)
   105	    this.header_list = []
   106	    this.#options = options
   107	  }
   108	
   109	  parse(lines) {
   110	    for (const line of lines) {
   111	      if (/^[ \t]/.test(line)) {
   112	        this.header_list[this.header_list.length - 1] += line
   113	      } else {
   114	        this.header_list.push(line)
   115	      }
   116	    }
   117	
   118	    for (const header of this.header_list) {
   119	      const match = header.match(/^([^\s:]*):\s*([\s\S]*)$/)
   120	      if (match) {
   121	        this._add_header(match[1].toLowerCase(), match[2], 'push')
   122	      } else {
   123	        logger.lognotice(`Header did not look right: ${header}`)
   124	      }
   125	    }
   126	
   127	    for (const key of Object.keys(this.headers)) {
   128	      for (const val of this.headers[key]) {
   129	        this._add_header_decode(key, val, 'push')
   130	      }
   131	    }
   132	  }
   133	
   134	  decode_header(val) {
   135	    val = val.replace(/\r?\n/g, '')
   136	
   137	    const rfc2231_params = {
   138	      kv: {},
   139	      keys: {},
   140	      cur_key: '',
   141	      cur_enc: '',
   142	      cur_lang: '',
   143	    }
   144	
   145	    val = _decode_rfc2231(rfc2231_params, val)
   146	
   147	    if (!/^[\x00-\x7f]*$/.test(val)) {
   148	      const matches = /\bcharset\s*=\s*["']?([\w_-]*)/.exec(
   149	        this.get('content-type'),
   150	      )
   151	      if (matches && !/UTF-?8/i.test(matches[1])) {
   152	        val = try_convert(Buffer.from(val, 'binary'), matches[1])
   153	      }
   154	    }
   155	
   156	    if (!/=\?/.test(val)) return val
   157	
   158	    return val
   159	      .replace(/(=\?.+?\?=)\s+(?==\?.+?\?=)/g, '$1')
   160	      .replace(
   161	        /=\?([\w_-]+)(\*[\w_-]+)?\?([bqBQ])\?([\s\S]*?)\?=/g,
   162	        _decode_header,
   163	      )
   164	  }
   165	
   166	  get(key) {
   167	    return (this.headers[key.toLowerCase()] ?? []).join('\n')
   168	  }
   169	
   170	  get_all(key) {
   171	    return Object.freeze([...(this.headers[key.toLowerCase()] ?? [])])
   172	  }
   173	
   174	  get_decoded(key) {
   175	    return (this.headers_decoded[key.toLowerCase()] ?? []).join('\n')
   176	  }
   177	
   178	  remove(key) {
   179	    key = key.toLowerCase()
   180	    delete this.headers[key]
   181	    delete this.headers_decoded[key]
   182	    this._remove_more(key)
   183	  }
   184	
   185	  _remove_more(key) {
   186	    const key_len = key.length
   187	    for (let i = 0, l = this.header_list.length; i < l; i++) {
   188	      if (
   189	        this.header_list[i].substring(0, key_len + 1).toLowerCase() ===
   190	        `${key}:`
   191	      ) {
   192	        this.header_list.splice(i, 1)
   193	        return this._remove_more(key)
   194	      }
   195	    }
   196	  }
   197	
   198	  add(key, value) {
   199	    if (!key) key = 'X-Haraka-Blank'
   200	    value = trimNewlines(value)
   201	    if (/[^\x00-\x7f]/.test(value)) value = libmime.encodeWords(value, 'Q')
   202	    this._add_header(key.toLowerCase(), value, 'unshift')
   203	    this._add_header_decode(key.toLowerCase(), value, 'unshift')
   204	    this.header_list.unshift(`${key}: ${value}\n`)
   205	  }
   206	
   207	  add_end(key, value) {
   208	    if (!key) key = 'X-Haraka-Blank'
   209	    value = trimNewlines(value)
   210	    if (/[^\x00-\x7f]/.test(value)) value = libmime.encodeWords(value, 'Q')
   211	    this._add_header(key.toLowerCase(), value, 'push')
   212	    this._add_header_decode(key.toLowerCase(), value, 'push')
   213	    this.header_list.push(`${key}: ${value}\n`)
   214	  }
   215	
   216	  _add_header(key, value, method) {
   217	    if (['__proto__', 'constructor', 'prototype'].includes(key)) return
   218	    this.headers[key] ??= []
   219	    this.headers[key][method](value)
   220	  }
   221	
   222	  _add_header_decode(key, value, method) {
   223	    if (['__proto__', 'constructor', 'prototype'].includes(key)) return
   224	    this.headers_decoded[key] ??= []
   225	    this.headers_decoded[key][method](this.decode_header(value))
   226	  }
   227	
   228	  lines() {
   229	    return Object.freeze([...this.header_list])
   230	  }
   231	
   232	  toString() {
   233	    return this.header_list.join('\n')
   234	  }
   235	}
   236	
   237	function trimNewlines(value) {
   238	  // 4B ops/sec vs regex at 1.4M ops/sec
   239	  while (value.length > 0) {
   240	    if (value.endsWith('\n')) {
   241	      if (value.endsWith('\r\n')) {
   242	        value = value.slice(0, -2)
   243	      } else {
   244	        value = value.slice(0, -1)
   245	      }
   246	    } else {
   247	      break // Stop at a stray \r or any other character
   248	    }
   249	  }
   250	  return value
   251	}
   252	
   253	module.exports = Header
   254

     2	
     3	const fs = require('node:fs')
     4	const path = require('node:path')
     5	const { Stream, PassThrough, Writable } = require('node:stream')
     6	
     7	const ChunkEmitter = require('./lib/chunk-emitter')
     8	const HeaderSkipper = require('./lib/header-skipper')
     9	const LineTransformer = require('./lib/line-transformer')
    10	
    11	const STATE = { HEADERS: 1, BODY: 2 }
    12	const MAX_IDX_KEYS = 1000
    13	
    14	class MessageStream extends Stream {
    15	  // Public observable state
    16	  uuid
    17	  bytes_read = 0
    18	  state = STATE.HEADERS
    19	  idx = {}
    20	  spooling = false
    21	  buffered = 0
    22	  total_buffered = 0
    23	  max_data_inflight = 0
    24	  headers
    25	
    26	  // Private write-side state
    27	  #queue = []
    28	  #writeCe = null
    29	  #fd = null
    30	  #ws = null
    31	  #openPending = false
    32	  #writePending = false
    33	  #writeComplete = false
    34	  #wsEnded = false
    35	  #endCalled = false
    36	  #endCallback = null
    37	  #idxCount = 0
    38	
    39	  // Private read-side state
    40	  #inPipe = false
    41	  #currentSource = null
    42	
    43	  // Config
    44	  #bufferMax
    45	  #spoolDir
    46	  #filename
    47	
    48	  constructor(cfg = {}, id, headers) {
    49	    super()
    50	    if (!id) throw new Error('id required')
    51	    this.uuid = id
    52	    this.headers = headers ?? []
    53	    this.#bufferMax = !isNaN(cfg?.main?.spool_after)
    54	      ? Number(cfg.main.spool_after)
    55	      : -1
    56	    this.#spoolDir = cfg?.main?.spool_dir ?? '/tmp'
    57	    this.#filename = path.join(this.#spoolDir, `${path.basename(id)}.eml`)
    58	  }
    59	
    60	  // ── Write side ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    61	
    62	  add_line(line) {
    63	    if (typeof line === 'string') line = Buffer.from(line)
    64	
    65	    if (!this.#writeCe) {
    66	      this.#writeCe = new ChunkEmitter()
    67	      this.#writeCe.on('data', (chunk) => this.#write(chunk))
    68	    }
    69	
    70	    this.bytes_read += line.length
    71	
    72	    // Build up an index of 'interesting' data on the fly
    73	    if (this.state === STATE.HEADERS) {
    74	      // Look for end of headers line
    75	      if (line.length === 2 && line[0] === 0x0d && line[1] === 0x0a) {
    76	        this.idx.headers = { start: 0, end: this.bytes_read - line.length }
    77	        this.#idxCount++
    78	        this.state = STATE.BODY
    79	        this.idx.body = { start: this.bytes_read }
    80	        this.#idxCount++
    81	      }
    82	    }
    83	
    84	    if (this.state === STATE.BODY) {
    85	      // Look for MIME boundaries
    86	      if (line.length > 4 && line[0] === 0x2d && line[1] === 0x2d) {
    87	        let boundary = line.slice(2).toString().trimEnd()
    88	        if (/--\s*$/.test(line)) {
    89	          boundary = boundary.slice(0, -2)
    90	          if (this.idx[boundary]) this.idx[boundary].end = this.bytes_read
    91	        } else {
    92	          if (!this.idx[boundary] && this.#idxCount < MAX_IDX_KEYS) {
    93	            this.idx[boundary] = { start: this.bytes_read - line.length }
    94	            this.#idxCount++
    95	          }
    96	        }
    97	      }
    98	    }
    99	
   100	    this.#writeCe.fill(line)
   101	  }
   102	
   103	  add_line_end(cb) {
   104	    if (this.idx.body) this.idx.body.end = this.bytes_read
   105	    this.#endCalled = true
   106	    if (typeof cb === 'function') this.#endCallback = cb
   107	    // Call _write() only if no new data was emitted This might happen if the
   108	    // message size matches the size of the chunk buffer.
   109	    if (!this.#writeCe.end()) this.#write()
   110	  }
   111	
   112	  #write(data) {
   113	    if (data) {
   114	      this.buffered += data.length
   115	      this.total_buffered += data.length
   116	      this.#queue.push(data)
   117	    }
   118	    if (this.buffered > this.max_data_inflight)
   119	      this.max_data_inflight = this.buffered
   120	
   121	    if (this.#openPending || this.#writePending) return false
   122	
   123	    if (this.#bufferMax !== -1 && this.total_buffered > this.#bufferMax)
   124	      this.spooling = true
   125	
   126	    if (this.#endCalled && (!this.spooling || !this.#queue.length)) {
   127	      if (this.spooling && this.#ws && !this.#wsEnded) {
   128	        // SUNSET 2027 (node 20 exits LTS on 2026-04-18)
   129	        // see test: 'spool file is fully written before add_line_end callback fires'
   130	        this.#wsEnded = true
   131	        this.#ws.end(() => {
   132	          this.#endCallback?.()
   133	          if (!this.#writeComplete) {
   134	            this.#writeComplete = true
   135	            this.emit('_write_complete')
   136	          }
   137	        })
   138	      } else if (!this.#wsEnded) {
   139	        this.#endCallback?.()
   140	        if (!this.#writeComplete) {
   141	          this.#writeComplete = true
   142	          this.emit('_write_complete')
   143	        }
   144	      }
   145	      return true
   146	    }
   147	
   148	    if (
   149	      this.#bufferMax === -1 ||
   150	      (this.buffered < this.#bufferMax && !this.spooling)
   151	    ) {
   152	      return true
   153	    }
   154	
   155	    if (!this.#queue.length) return false
   156	
   157	    // Open spool file if needed
   158	    if (!this.#fd && !this.#openPending) {
   159	      this.#openPending = true
   160	      this.#ws = fs.createWriteStream(this.#filename, {
   161	        flags: 'wx+',
   162	        autoClose: false,
   163	      })
   164	      this.#ws.on('open', (fd) => {
   165	        this.#fd = fd
   166	        this.#openPending = false
   167	        setImmediate(() => this.#write())
   168	      })
   169	      this.#ws.on('error', (err) => this.emit('error', err))
   170	    }
   171	
   172	    if (!this.#fd) return false
   173	
   174	    const toSend = this.#queue.shift()
   175	    this.buffered -= toSend.length
   176	
   177	    if (!this.#ws.write(toSend)) {
   178	      this.#writePending = true
   179	      this.#ws.once('drain', () => {
   180	        this.#writePending = false
   181	        setImmediate(() => this.#write())
   182	      })
   183	      return false
   184	    }
   185	
   186	    if (this.#endCalled && (!this.spooling || !this.#queue.length))
   187	      return this.#write()
   188	    return true
   189	  }
   190	
   191	  // ── Read side ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   192	
   193	  pipe(destination, options = {}) {
   194	    if (this.#inPipe) throw new Error('Cannot pipe while currently piping')
   195	
   196	    const lineEndings = options?.line_endings ?? '\r\n'
   197	    const skipHeaders = !!options?.skip_headers
   198	
   199	    this.#inPipe = true
   200	
   201	    const transformer = new LineTransformer({
   202	      lineEndings,
   203	      dotStuffed: options?.dot_stuffed ?? true,
   204	      endingDot: options?.ending_dot ?? false,
   205	      clamdStyle: !!options?.clamd_style,
   206	    })
   207	    const source = new PassThrough()
   208	    this.#currentSource = source
   209	
   210	    source.pipe(transformer).pipe(destination, { end: options.end !== false })
   211	
   212	    transformer.once('end', () => {
   213	      this.#inPipe = false
   214	    })
   215	    transformer.once('error', (err) => {
   216	      this.#inPipe = false
   217	      this.emit('error', err)
   218	    })
   219	    source.once('error', (err) => this.emit('error', err))
   220	
   221	    // Constructor headers suppress raw-data headers; skip_headers also suppresses ctor headers
   222	    const emitCtorHeaders = this.headers.length > 0 && !skipHeaders
   223	    const skipRawHeaders = this.headers.length > 0 || skipHeaders
   224	
   225	    const doRead = () => {
   226	      if (emitCtorHeaders) {
   227	        for (const h of this.headers) {
   228	          source.write(Buffer.from(h.replace(/\r?\n/g, lineEndings)))
   229	        }
   230	        source.write(Buffer.from(lineEndings)) // EOH marker
   231	      }
   232	
   233	      if (this.#queue.length > 0) {
   234	        // In-memory path
   235	        if (skipRawHeaders) {
   236	          const skipper = new HeaderSkipper()
   237	          skipper.on('error', (err) => source.destroy(err))
   238	          skipper.pipe(source)
   239	          for (const chunk of this.#queue) skipper.write(chunk.slice(0))
   240	          skipper.end()
   241	        } else {
   242	          for (const chunk of this.#queue) source.write(chunk.slice(0))
   243	          source.end()
   244	        }
   245	      } else {
   246	        // Disk path
   247	        const startRead = (fd) => {
   248	          const rs = fs.createReadStream(null, { fd, start: 0 })
   249	          // Prevent the fd from being closed so it can be reused across sequential pipes
   250	          rs.destroy = () => {}
   251	          rs.once('error', (err) => {
   252	            source.destroy(err)
   253	            this.emit('error', err)
   254	          })
   255	          if (skipRawHeaders) {
   256	            const skipper = new HeaderSkipper()
   257	            skipper.on('error', (err) => source.destroy(err))
   258	            rs.pipe(skipper).pipe(source)
   259	          } else {
   260	            rs.pipe(source)
   261	          }
   262	        }
   263	
   264	        if (this.#fd) {
   265	          startRead(this.#fd)
   266	        } else {
   267	          fs.open(this.#filename, 'r', (err, fd) => {
   268	            if (err) {
   269	              this.emit('error', err)
   270	              source.destroy(err)
   271	              return
   272	            }
   273	            this.#fd = fd
   274	            startRead(fd)
   275	          })
   276	        }
   277	      }
   278	    }
   279	
   280	    if (this.#writeComplete) {
   281	      doRead()
   282	    } else {
   283	      this.once('_write_complete', doRead)
   284	    }
   285	
   286	    return destination
   287	  }
   288	
   289	  pause() {
   290	    this.#currentSource?.pause()
   291	  }
   292	
   293	  resume() {
   294	    this.#currentSource?.resume()
   295	  }
   296	
   297	  destroy() {
   298	    try {
   299	      if (this.#fd) {
   300	        fs.close(this.#fd, () => fs.unlink(this.#filename, () => {}))
   301	      } else {
   302	        fs.unlink(this.#filename, () => {})
   303	      }
   304	    } catch {
   305	      // ignore
   306	    }
   307	  }
   308	
   309	  get_data(options, cb) {
   310	    if (arguments.length === 1) {
   311	      cb = options
   312	      options = {}
   313	    }
   314	    this.pipe(new GetDataStream(cb), options)
   315	  }
   316	
   317	  [Symbol.asyncIterator](options = {}) {
   318	    const pass = new PassThrough()
   319	    this.pipe(pass, options)
   320	    return pass[Symbol.asyncIterator]()
   321	  }
   322	}
   323	
   324	module.exports = MessageStream
   325	
   326	class GetDataStream extends Writable {
   327	  constructor(cb) {
   328	    super()
   329	    this.cb = cb
   330	    this.chunks = []
   331	  }
   332	
   333	  _write(chunk, _enc, done) {
   334	    this.chunks.push(chunk)
   335	    done()
   336	  }
   337	
   338	  _final(done) {
   339	    this.cb(Buffer.concat(this.chunks))
   340	    done()
   341	  }
   342	}
   343	
   344	module.exports.ChunkEmitter = ChunkEmitter
   345

   284	            { desc: 'multi-line with dots', input: 'a\n.b\n', expected: 'a\r\n..b\r\n' },

   286	        ]

   291	                const result = this.transaction.add_dot_stuffing_and_ensure_crlf_newlines(inBuf)

Now I understand the structure. The issue is about `txn.add_header` not correctly appending headers in the right place. Let me trace the flow:

1. `add_header(key, value)` calls `this.header.add_end(key, value)` which pushes to `header_list` 
2. Then calls `this.reset_headers()` which gets lines from `this.header.lines()` and sets `this.header_pos = header_lines.length`

But the problem is: `reset_headers()` only updates `this.header_pos`. It doesn't update the message stream. The message stream was already set up with the original headers from `this.header.header_list` (passed by reference in the constructor).

Looking at line 29:
```js
this.message_stream = new message.stream(this.cfg, this.uuid, this.header.header_list)
```

The message stream stores a reference to `this.header.header_list`. When `add_header` adds to the header list via `this.header.add_end()`, it modifies the `header_list` array in place. So the message stream should see the updated headers. Let me verify this...

Actually, wait. Looking at `header.add_end()`:
```js
add_end(key, value) {
    ...
    this.header_list.push(`${key}: ${value}\n`)
}
```

This pushes to the array. And `message.stream` constructor receives `this.header.header_list` which is a reference to the same array. So the message stream should include the new header.

But let me look more carefully at how the message stream handles the headers when it pipes:

In `MessageStream.pipe()`:
```js
// Constructor headers suppress raw-data headers; skip_headers also suppresses ctor headers
const emitCtorHeaders = this.headers.length > 0 && !skipHeaders
const skipRawHeaders = this.headers.length > 0 || skipHeaders
```

So if `this.headers` (which is the header_list) has items, it emits those constructor headers AND skips the raw data headers. The issue is likely that the headers are emitted, then an empty line (EOH marker), and then the raw headers are skipped and the body follows. 

But wait, the problem in the PR description is about headers ending up at the end of the message body. Let me think about when this could happen.

The issue is: when `add_header` is called AFTER the data has been added but before the message is sent. Let's trace the flow:

1. Data is received, headers are collected in `header_lines`, and the message stream receives lines via `add_line`.
2. At some point, `add_header` is called. This adds to `this.header.header_list` and calls `reset_headers()` which updates `this.header_pos`.
3. But the message stream has already been receiving lines. The message stream's `state` has already transitioned to BODY.

The problem is: the message stream's internal buffer (`#queue`) already contains the original headers and body. The header_list was modified, but the message stream's queued data still has the old headers. When the message is piped out, the message stream will emit:
- Constructor headers (from `this.headers`, which includes the newly added header)
- Blank line
- Skip raw headers (skip the original headers that are in the queue)
- Body

But actually, looking at the pipe method more carefully:

```js
if (emitCtorHeaders) {
    for (const h of this.headers) {
        source.write(Buffer.from(h.replace(/\r?\n/g, lineEndings)))
    }
    source.write(Buffer.from(lineEndings)) // EOH marker
}
```

So it writes the constructor headers and then an EOH marker (blank line). Then if `skipRawHeaders` is true, it uses a HeaderSkipper to skip the original headers from the queue. This should work correctly...

Unless the issue is that `add_header` is called BEFORE the headers have been parsed (i.e., before `header_pos > 0`). Let me look at the condition:

```js
add_header(key, value) {
    this.header.add_end(key, value)
    if (this.header_pos > 0) this.reset_headers()
}
```

If `header_pos === 0`, it doesn't call `reset_headers()`. That means the header was added to `header_list` but `header_pos` stays 0. The `reset_headers()` method just updates `header_pos`:

```js
reset_headers() {
    const header_lines = this.header.lines()
    this.header_pos = header_lines.length
}
```

So if `header_pos > 0`, it just sets it to the new length. But if `header_pos === 0`, nothing happens. But the header was still added to the list, and since the message stream references the same list, it should still work.

Wait, let me look at the PR description again. The issue is about headers ending up at the end of the message body. Let me look at the actual email in the description: the headers like `X-Spam-Status`, `DKIM-Signature` etc. appear AFTER the body text. This suggests that when headers are appended, they're being placed after the body content instead of in the header section.

Let me think about this differently. Maybe the problem is in how the message stream handles the case when headers are added after some data has already been written to the message stream.

     1	'use strict'
     2	
     3	const { Transform } = require('node:stream')
     4	
     5	const MAX_HEADER_SIZE = 1024 * 1024 // 1MB
     6	
     7	function indexOfLF(buf) {
     8	  for (let i = 0; i < buf.length; i++) {
     9	    if (buf[i] === 0x0a) return i
    10	  }
    11	  return -1
    12	}
    13	
    14	// Strips everything from the start of a stored message up to and including
    15	// the blank EOH line (\r\n or \n), then passes body bytes through unchanged.
    16	class HeaderSkipper extends Transform {
    17	  #remainder = Buffer.alloc(0)
    18	  #eohFound = false
    19	
    20	  _transform(chunk, enc, cb) {
    21	    if (this.#eohFound) {
    22	      this.push(chunk)
    23	      return cb()
    24	    }
    25	
    26	    if (this.#remainder.length + chunk.length > MAX_HEADER_SIZE) {
    27	      return cb(new Error('Header size limit exceeded'))
    28	    }
    29	
    30	    let buf = Buffer.concat([this.#remainder, chunk])
    31	    let offset
    32	    while ((offset = indexOfLF(buf)) !== -1) {
    33	      const line = buf.slice(0, offset + 1)
    34	      buf = buf.slice(offset + 1)
    35	      const isEoh =
    36	        (line.length === 2 && line[0] === 0x0d && line[1] === 0x0a) ||
    37	        (line.length === 1 && line[0] === 0x0a)
    38	      if (isEoh) {
    39	        this.#eohFound = true
    40	        if (buf.length > 0) this.push(buf)
    41	        this.#remainder = Buffer.alloc(0)
    42	        return cb()
    43	      }
    44	    }
    45	    this.#remainder = buf
    46	    cb()
    47	  }
    48	
    49	  _flush(cb) {
    50	    // Any remainder after EOF with no EOH found is discarded (headers-only msg edge case)
    51	    if (this.#remainder.length > 0 && this.#eohFound) this.push(this.#remainder)
    52	    cb()
    53	  }
    54	}
    55	
    56	module.exports = HeaderSkipper
    57

     1	'use strict'
     2	
     3	const { EventEmitter } = require('node:events')
     4	
     5	class ChunkEmitter extends EventEmitter {
     6	  constructor(buffer_size) {
     7	    super()
     8	    this.buffer_size = parseInt(buffer_size) || 64 * 1024
     9	    this.buf = null
    10	    this.pos = 0
    11	    this.bufs = []
    12	    this.bufs_size = 0
    13	  }
    14	
    15	  fill(input) {
    16	    if (typeof input === 'string') input = Buffer.from(input)
    17	
    18	    if (!this.buf) {
    19	      this.bufs.push(input)
    20	      this.bufs_size += input.length
    21	      if (input.length + this.bufs_size > this.buffer_size) {
    22	        this.buf = Buffer.alloc(this.buffer_size)
    23	        const in_new = Buffer.concat(this.bufs, this.bufs_size)
    24	        input = in_new
    25	        this.bufs = []
    26	        this.bufs_size = 0
    27	      } else {
    28	        return
    29	      }
    30	    }
    31	
    32	    while (input.length > 0) {
    33	      let remaining = this.buffer_size - this.pos
    34	      if (remaining === 0) {
    35	        this.emit('data', this.buf)
    36	        this.buf = Buffer.alloc(this.buffer_size)
    37	        this.pos = 0
    38	        remaining = this.buffer_size
    39	      }
    40	      const to_write = remaining > input.length ? input.length : remaining
    41	      input.copy(this.buf, this.pos, 0, to_write)
    42	      this.pos += to_write
    43	      input = input.slice(to_write)
    44	    }
    45	  }
    46	
    47	  end(cb) {
    48	    let emitted = false
    49	    if (this.bufs_size > 0) {
    50	      this.emit('data', Buffer.concat(this.bufs, this.bufs_size))
    51	      emitted = true
    52	    } else if (this.pos > 0) {
    53	      this.emit('data', this.buf.slice(0, this.pos))
    54	      emitted = true
    55	    }
    56	    this.buf = null
    57	    this.pos = 0
    58	    this.bufs = []
    59	    this.bufs_size = 0
    60	    if (typeof cb === 'function') cb()
    61	    return emitted
    62	  }
    63	}
    64	
    65	module.exports = ChunkEmitter
    66

     1	'use strict'
     2	
     3	const { Transform } = require('node:stream')
     4	
     5	function indexOfLF(buf) {
     6	  for (let i = 0; i < buf.length; i++) {
     7	    if (buf[i] === 0x0a) return i
     8	  }
     9	  return -1
    10	}
    11	
    12	// Per-line transformer: dot-unstuffing, line-ending normalisation,
    13	// optional ending-dot, optional clamd-style size framing.
    14	class LineTransformer extends Transform {
    15	  #remainder = Buffer.alloc(0)
    16	  #lineEndings
    17	  #dotStuffed
    18	  #endingDot
    19	  #clamdStyle
    20	
    21	  constructor({
    22	    lineEndings = '\r\n',
    23	    dotStuffed = true,
    24	    endingDot = false,
    25	    clamdStyle = false,
    26	  } = {}) {
    27	    super()
    28	    this.#lineEndings = lineEndings
    29	    this.#dotStuffed = dotStuffed
    30	    this.#endingDot = endingDot
    31	    this.#clamdStyle = clamdStyle
    32	  }
    33	
    34	  #pushLine(line) {
    35	    if (this.#clamdStyle) {
    36	      const buf = Buffer.allocUnsafe(line.length + 4)
    37	      buf.writeUInt32BE(line.length, 0)
    38	      line.copy(buf, 4)
    39	      this.push(buf)
    40	    } else {
    41	      this.push(line)
    42	    }
    43	  }
    44	
    45	  #transformLine(line) {
    46	    // Remove SMTP dot-stuffing: leading '..' → '.'
    47	    if (
    48	      this.#dotStuffed &&
    49	      line.length >= 2 &&
    50	      line[0] === 0x2e &&
    51	      line[1] === 0x2e
    52	    ) {
    53	      line = line.slice(1)
    54	    }
    55	    // Strip \r when consumer wants bare-LF line endings
    56	    if (
    57	      this.#lineEndings === '\n' &&
    58	      line.length >= 2 &&
    59	      line[line.length - 1] === 0x0a &&
    60	      line[line.length - 2] === 0x0d
    61	    ) {
    62	      const copy = Buffer.allocUnsafe(line.length - 1)
    63	      line.copy(copy, 0, 0, line.length - 2)
    64	      copy[copy.length - 1] = 0x0a
    65	      line = copy
    66	    }
    67	    this.#pushLine(line)
    68	  }
    69	
    70	  _transform(chunk, enc, cb) {
    71	    let buf = Buffer.concat([this.#remainder, chunk])
    72	    let offset
    73	    while ((offset = indexOfLF(buf)) !== -1) {
    74	      this.#transformLine(buf.slice(0, offset + 1))
    75	      buf = buf.slice(offset + 1)
    76	    }
    77	    this.#remainder = buf
    78	    cb()
    79	  }
    80	
    81	  _flush(cb) {
    82	    if (this.#remainder.length > 0) {
    83	      this.#transformLine(this.#remainder)
    84	      this.#remainder = Buffer.alloc(0)
    85	    }
    86	    if (this.#endingDot) {
    87	      this.#pushLine(Buffer.from(`.${this.#lineEndings}`))
    88	    }
    89	    if (this.#clamdStyle) {
    90	      const term = Buffer.alloc(4)
    91	      term.writeUInt32BE(0, 0)
    92	      this.push(term)
    93	    }
    94	    cb()
    95	  }
    96	}
    97	
    98	module.exports = LineTransformer
    99

'use strict'

const config = require('haraka-config')
const transaction = require('./transaction')

// Create a transaction
const txn = transaction.createTransaction('test-uuid', config.get('smtp.ini'))

// Simulate adding headers and body data
txn.add_data('From: test@example.com\n')
txn.add_data('To: recipient@example.com\n')
txn.add_data('Subject: Test Subject\n')
txn.add_data('\n')
txn.add_data('This is the body.\n')

// Now end the data and then add a header (as would happen in a plugin)
txn.end_data(() => {
    txn.add_header('X-Appended-Header', 'test-value')
    
    // Now get the data and check where the header appears
    txn.message_stream.get_data((body) => {
        const bodyStr = body.toString()
        console.log('=== Full message: ===')
        console.log(bodyStr)
        console.log('=== End of message ===')
        
        // Check if the header appears after the body
        const headerPos = bodyStr.indexOf('X-Appended-Header')
        const bodyPos = bodyStr.indexOf('This is the body')
        
        console.log(`Header position: ${headerPos}`)
        console.log(`Body text position: ${bodyPos}`)
        
        if (headerPos > bodyPos) {
            console.log('BUG: Header appears AFTER body text!')
            process.exit(1)
        } else {
            console.log('OK: Header appears before body text')
            process.exit(0)
        }
    })
})
EOF

cd /testbed && cat > /tmp/repro.js << 'EOF'
'use strict'

const config = require('haraka-config')
const transaction = require('./transaction')

// Create a transaction
const txn = transaction.createTransaction('test-uuid', config.get('smtp.ini'))

// Simulate adding headers and body data
txn.add_data('From: test@example.com\n')
txn.add_data('To: recipient@example.com\n')
txn.add_data('Subject: Test Subject\n')
txn.add_data('\n')
txn.add_data('This is the body.\n')

// Now end the data and then add a header (as would happen in a plugin)
txn.end_data(() => {
    txn.add_header('X-Appended-Header', 'test-value')
    
    // Now get the data and check where the header appears
    txn.message_stream.get_data((body) => {
        const bodyStr = body.toString()
        console.log('=== Full message: ===')
        console.log(bodyStr)
        console.log('=== End of message ===')
        
        // Check if the header appears after the body
        const headerPos = bodyStr.indexOf('X-Appended-Header')
        const bodyPos = bodyStr.indexOf('This is the body')
        
        console.log(`Header position: ${headerPos}`)
        console.log(`Body text position: ${bodyPos}`)
        
        if (headerPos > bodyPos) {
            console.log('BUG: Header appears AFTER body text!')
            process.exit(1)
        } else {
            console.log('OK: Header appears before body text')
            process.exit(0)
        }
    })
})
EOF

2026-05-30 07:22:36,921 - INFO - swea-agent-haraka__Haraka-3535 - 💭 THOUGHT
Good, the tests pass. Now let me write a better reproduction script that directly tests the issue. The problem described in the PR is that appended headers sometimes end up after the body. Let me think about when this could happen...

Looking at the message stream code more carefully:

1. `add_line` is called for each line, building up a queue of chunks
2. `add_header` modifies `header_list` in place
3. When `pipe()` is called, if `this.headers.length > 0` (constructor headers), it emits those headers and then skips the raw headers using `HeaderSkipper`

The problem scenario might be:
- Headers are added via `add_header` before `end_data` is called
- But the message stream has already processed the original headers and body into chunks
- When piping, the constructor headers are emitted (which include the new appended headers), then the raw headers are skipped, and the body follows

That should work correctly... unless the issue is something else.

Let me look at the `add_header` method more carefully. It calls `this.header.add_end(key, value)` and then `if (this.header_pos > 0) this.reset_headers()`. But what about the message stream? The message stream has a `headers` property that was initialized with `this.header.header_list` (the array reference). Since `add_end` pushes to the array, the message stream should see the new header.

Wait, let me re-read the `MessageStream` constructor:

```js
constructor(cfg = {}, id, headers) {
    ...
    this.headers = headers ?? []
    ...
}
```

And in Transaction:
```js
this.message_stream = new message.stream(this.cfg, this.uuid, this.header.header_list)
```

So `this.message_stream.headers` IS `this.header.header_list`. They're the same array. When `add_end` pushes to `this.header_list`, it also affects `this.message_stream.headers`. So when the message is piped, it should include the new header.

But wait - the problem might be when `add_header` is called BEFORE the header/body separator is found (i.e., `header_pos === 0`). In that case, `reset_headers()` is not called. But the header is still added to the list. However, the raw data being added via `add_data` still contains the original headers without the appended one. When the message is finally assembled:
1. Constructor headers (including the appended one) are emitted
2. Blank line
3. Raw headers are skipped
4. Body follows

This seems correct. So what's the actual bug?

Let me look at the `haraka-test-fixtures` for how transaction is tested there

    11	  constructor(uuid, cfg) {
    12	    this.uuid = uuid || '111111-222222-333-4444444'
    13	    this.cfg = cfg

    26	    this.notes = new Notes()

    30	    this.discard_data = false

    33	      accept: 0,

    39	    this.data_post_delay = 0
    40	    this.encoding = 'utf8'
    41	    this.mime_part_count = 0
    42	  }
    43	
    44	  ensure_body() {
    45	    if (this.body) return
    46	
    47	    this.body = new message.Body(this.header)
    48	    for (const hook of this.attachment_start_hooks) {
    49	      this.body.on('attachment_start', hook)
    50	    }
    51	
    52	    if (this.banner) this.body.set_banner(this.banner)
    53	
    54	    for (const o of this.body_filters) {
    55	      this.body.add_filter((ct, enc, buf) => {
    56	        const re_match = util.types.isRegExp(o.ct_match) && o.ct_match.test(ct.toLowerCase())
    57	        const ct_begins = ct.toLowerCase().indexOf(String(o.ct_match).toLowerCase()) === 0
    58	        if (re_match || ct_begins) return o.filter(ct, enc, buf)
    59	      })
    60	    }
    61	  }
    62	
    63	  // Removes the CR of a CRLF newline at the end of the buffer.
    64	  remove_final_cr(data) {
    65	    if (data.length < 2) return data
    66	    if (!Buffer.isBuffer(data)) data = Buffer.from(data)
    67	
    68	    if (data[data.length - 2] === 0x0d && data[data.length - 1] === 0x0a) {
    69	      data[data.length - 2] = 0x0a
    70	      return data.slice(0, data.length - 1)
    71	    }
    72	    return data
    73	  }
    74	
    75	  // Duplicates any '.' chars at the beginning of a line (dot-stuffing) and
    76	  // ensures all newlines are CRLF.
    77	  add_dot_stuffing_and_ensure_crlf_newlines(data) {
    78	    if (!data.length) return data
    79	    if (!Buffer.isBuffer(data)) data = Buffer.from(data)
    80	
    81	    // Make a new buffer big enough to hold two bytes for every one input
    82	    // byte.  At most, we add one extra character per input byte, so this
    83	    // is always big enough.  We allocate it "unsafe" (i.e. no memset) for
    84	    // speed because we're about to fill it with data, and the remainder of
    85	    // the space we don't fill will be sliced away before we return this.
    86	    const output = Buffer.allocUnsafe(data.length * 2)
    87	    let output_pos = 0
    88	
    89	    let input_pos = 0
    90	    let next_dot = data.indexOf(0x2e)
    91	    let next_lf = data.indexOf(0x0a)
    92	    while (next_dot !== -1 || next_lf !== -1) {
    93	      const run_end = next_dot !== -1 && (next_lf === -1 || next_dot < next_lf) ? next_dot : next_lf
    94	
    95	      // Copy up till whichever comes first, '.' or '\n' (but don't
    96	      // copy the '.' or '\n' itself).
    97	      data.copy(output, output_pos, input_pos, run_end)
    98	      output_pos += run_end - input_pos
    99	
   100	      if (data[run_end] === 0x2e && (run_end === 0 || data[run_end - 1] === 0x0a)) {
   101	        // Replace /^\./ with '..'
   102	        output[output_pos++] = 0x2e
   103	      } else if (data[run_end] === 0x0a && (run_end === 0 || data[run_end - 1] !== 0x0d)) {
   104	        // Replace /\r?\n/ with '\r\n'
   105	        output[output_pos++] = 0x0d
   106	      }
   107	      output[output_pos++] = data[run_end]
   108	
   109	      input_pos = run_end + 1
   110	
   111	      if (run_end === next_dot) {
   112	        next_dot = data.indexOf(0x2e, input_pos)
   113	      } else {
   114	        next_lf = data.indexOf(0x0a, input_pos)
   115	      }
   116	    }
   117	
   118	    if (input_pos < data.length) {
   119	      data.copy(output, output_pos, input_pos)
   120	      output_pos += data.length - input_pos
   121	    }
   122	
   123	    return output.slice(0, output_pos)
   124	  }
   125	
   126	  add_data(line) {
   127	    if (typeof line === 'string') {
   128	      // This shouldn't ever happen...
   129	      line = Buffer.from(line, this.encoding)
   130	    }
   131	    // is this the end of headers line?
   132	    if (this.header_pos === 0 && (line[0] === 0x0a || (line[0] === 0x0d && line[1] === 0x0a))) {
   133	      this.header.parse(this.header_lines)
   134	      this.header_pos = this.header_lines.length
   135	      this.found_hb_sep = true
   136	      if (this.parse_body) {
   137	        this.ensure_body()
   138	      }
   139	    } else if (this.header_pos === 0) {
   140	      // Build up headers
   141	      if (this.header_lines.length < this.cfg.headers.max_lines) {
   142	        if (line[0] === 0x2e) line = line.slice(1) // Strip leading "."
   143	        this.header_lines.push(line.toString(this.encoding).replace(/\r\n$/, '\n'))
   144	      }
   145	    } else if (this.parse_body) {
   146	      let new_line = line
   147	      if (new_line[0] === 0x2e) new_line = new_line.slice(1) // Strip leading "."
   148	
   149	      line = this.add_dot_stuffing_and_ensure_crlf_newlines(this.body.parse_more(this.remove_final_cr(new_line)))
   150	      if (!line.length) return // buffering for banners
   151	    }
   152	
   153	    if (!this.discard_data) this.message_stream.add_line(line)
   154	  }
   155	
   156	  end_data(cb) {
   157	    if (!this.found_hb_sep && this.header_lines.length) {
   158	      // Headers not parsed yet - must be a busted email
   159	      // Strategy: Find the first line that doesn't look like a header.
   160	      // Treat anything before that as headers, anything after as body.
   161	      let header_pos = 0
   162	      for (let i = 0; i < this.header_lines.length; i++) {
   163	        // Anything that doesn't match a header or continuation
   164	        if (!/^(?:([^\s:]*):\s*([\s\S]*)$|[ \t])/.test(this.header_lines[i])) {
   165	          break
   166	        }
   167	        header_pos = i
   168	      }
   169	      const body_lines = this.header_lines.splice(header_pos + 1)
   170	      this.header.parse(this.header_lines)
   171	      this.header_pos = header_pos
   172	      if (this.parse_body) {
   173	        this.ensure_body()
   174	        for (const bodyLine of body_lines) {
   175	          this.body.parse_more(bodyLine)
   176	        }
   177	      }
   178	    }
   179	    if (this.header_pos && this.parse_body) {
   180	      const line = this.add_dot_stuffing_and_ensure_crlf_newlines(this.body.parse_end())
   181	      if (line.length) {
   182	        this.body.force_end()
   183	
   184	        if (!this.discard_data) this.message_stream.add_line(line)
   185	      }
   186	    }
   187	
   188	    if (this.discard_data) {
   189	      cb()
   190	    } else {
   191	      this.message_stream.add_line_end(cb)
   192	    }
   193	  }
   194	
   195	  add_header(key, value) {
   196	    this.header.add_end(key, value)
   197	    if (this.header_pos > 0) this.reset_headers()
   198	  }
   199	
   200	  add_leading_header(key, value) {
   201	    this.header.add(key, value)
   202	    if (this.header_pos > 0) this.reset_headers()
   203	  }
   204	
   205	  reset_headers() {
   206	    const header_lines = this.header.lines()
   207	    this.header_pos = header_lines.length
   208	  }
   209	
   210	  remove_header(key) {
   211	    this.header.remove(key)
   212	    if (this.header_pos > 0) this.reset_headers()
   213	  }
   214	
   215	  attachment_hooks(start, data, end) {
   216	    this.parse_body = 1
   217	    this.attachment_start_hooks.push(start)
   218	  }
   219	
   220	  set_banner(text, html) {
   221	    // throw "transaction.set_banner is currently non-functional";
   222	    this.parse_body = true
   223	    if (!html) {
   224	      html = text.replace(/\n/g, '<br/>\n')
   225	    }
   226	    this.banner = [text, html]
   227	  }
   228	
   229	  add_body_filter(ct_match, filter) {
   230	    this.parse_body = true
   231	    this.body_filters.push({ ct_match, filter })
   232	  }
   233	
   234	  incr_mime_count() {
   235	    this.mime_part_count++
   236	  }
   237	}
   238	
   239	exports.Transaction = Transaction
   240	
   241	exports.createTransaction = function (uuid, cfg) {
   242	  const t = new Transaction(uuid, cfg)
   243	  logger.add_log_methods(t, 'mock-connection')
   244	  return t
   245	}

2026-05-30 07:23:13,178 - INFO - swea-agent-haraka__Haraka-3535 - 💭 THOUGHT
Interesting! The test fixture has a slightly different version. Notably, in the test fixture:

1. Line 141: `if (this.header_lines.length < this.cfg.headers.max_lines)` - uses `this.cfg.headers.max_lines` without optional chaining
2. Line 143: `this.header_lines.push(line.toString(this.encoding).replace(/\r\n$/, '\n'))` - doesn't have optional chaining on encoding

But the real transaction.js has:
1. Line 141: `if (this.header_lines.length < (this.cfg?.headers?.max_lines || 1000))` - uses optional chaining
2. Line 143: `this.header_lines.push(line.toString(this.encoding).replace(/\r\n$/, '\n'))` - uses `this.encoding`

But that's not the issue. Let me look more carefully at the flow.

The issue is that `add_header` modifies `this.header.header_list` but the message stream's internal queue already contains the raw headers and body. When the message is finally piped, the constructor headers are emitted (including the new header), then the raw headers are skipped, and the body follows. This should be correct.

But wait - there's a subtle issue! Look at how the message stream's `pipe()` works:

```js
const emitCtorHeaders = this.headers.length > 0 && !skipHeaders
const skipRawHeaders = this.headers.length > 0 || skipHeaders
```

If `this.headers` has items (which it always does because headers were added during `add_data`), then it emits constructor headers AND skips raw headers. But the constructor headers include ONLY what's in `this.headers` (the header_list). 

Now, the header_list is built up in TWO ways:
1. During `add_data`, the raw headers are parsed and added to `header_list` via `this.header.parse(this.header_lines)` at line 135.
2. When `add_header` is called, it adds to `header_list` via `this.header.add_end()`.

So if `add_header` is called AFTER the header/body separator is found (i.e., after `this.header.parse(this.header_lines)` has been called), the new header is added to `header_list` after the parsed headers. When the message is piped:
- The constructor headers are emitted (which include the new header)
- The raw headers are skipped
- Body follows

This seems correct.

But what if `add_header` is called BEFORE the header/body separator is found? In that case:
- `this.header.add_end(key, value)` adds to `header_list`
- `this.header_pos === 0`, so `reset_headers()` is NOT called
- Later, when the blank line is encountered, `this.header.parse(this.header_lines)` is called
- `header.parse` re-processes `this.header_lines` and calls `this._add_header(match[1].toLowerCase(), match[2], 'push')` for each line
- But wait, `add_end` already pushed to `header_list` and `headers`. Now `parse` will ADD more items to the same arrays. This could result in duplicates or ordering issues!

Actually, let me look at `header.parse()` more carefully:

```js
parse(lines) {
    for (const line of lines) {
        if (/^[ \t]/.test(line)) {
            this.header_list[this.header_list.length - 1] += line
        } else {
            this.header_list.push(line)
        }
    }
    
    for (const header of this.header_list) {
        const match = header.match(/^([^\s:]*):\s*([\s\S]*)$/)
        if (match) {
            this._add_header(match[1].toLowerCase(), match[2], 'push')
        }
    }
    ...
}
```

So `parse` pushes to `header_list` and then iterates over `header_list` to add to `headers`. If `add_end` was called before `parse`, then:
1. `add_end` pushes the new header to `header_list` 
2. `parse` pushes all raw headers to `header_list` (so the new header is at position 0, raw headers follow)
3. `parse` then iterates over ALL items in `header_list` (including the pre-added one) and adds to `headers`

Wait, but `add_end` adds to the END of `header_list` (push), while `parse` also adds to the end. So the order would be:
- After `add_end`: `header_list = ["X-Appended: value\n"]`
- After `parse` (first loop): `header_list = ["X-Appended: value\n", "From: ...\n", "To: ...\n", "Subject: ...\n"]`

That seems fine. The appended header would be first, then the original headers. But that's not right either - `add_header` should APPEND headers (at the end), not prepend them.

Wait, `add_end` uses `push` which adds to the end. So the new header should be at the end:
- After `add_end`: `header_list = ["X-Appended: value\n"]`
- After `parse` first loop: `header_list = ["X-Appended: value\n", "From: ...\n", ...]`

Hmm, that puts the appended header FIRST, which is wrong. But actually, `add_header` is typically called AFTER the data is received, so `header_list` already has items from `parse`:
- After `parse`: `header_list = ["From: ...\n", "To: ...\n", "Subject: ...\n"]`
- After `add_end`: `header_list = ["From: ...\n", "To: ...\n", "Subject: ...\n", "X-Appended: value\n"]`

That's correct! The appended header is at the end.

Now, what happens with the message stream? The message stream has already stored the raw data in its internal queue. The raw data includes the original headers and body. When piping:
- Constructor headers (from `header_list`) include the new header at the end
- Raw headers are skipped
- Body follows

This should work. So where's the bug?

Let me look at the PR description again. It says "Sometimes, appended headers aren't put in the right spot." The example shows headers like `X-Spam-Status`, `DKIM-Signature` appearing after the body text.

Wait, I think I need to look at the actual flow more carefully. The `add_header` is called from plugins. When is it called? It could be called at different hook points. Let me check the connection.js to see the context

  1530	        // Look for .\n
  1531	        if (line.length === 2 && line[0] === 0x2e && line[1] === 0x0a) {
  1532	            this.lognotice('Client sent bare line-feed - .\\n rather than .\\r\\n')
  1533	            this.respond(451, 'Bare line-feed; see http://haraka.github.io/barelf/', () => {
  1534	                this.reset_transaction()
  1535	            })
  1536	            return
  1537	        }
  1538	
  1539	        // Stop accumulating data as we're going to reject at dot.
  1540	        if (cfg.max.bytes && this.transaction.data_bytes > cfg.max.bytes) {
  1541	            return
  1542	        }
  1543	
  1544	        if (this.transaction.mime_part_count >= cfg.max.mime_parts) {
  1545	            this.logcrit('Possible DoS attempt - too many MIME parts')
  1546	            this.respond(554, 'Transaction failed due to too many MIME parts', () => {
  1547	                this.disconnect()
  1548	            })
  1549	            return
  1550	        }
  1551	
  1552	        this.transaction.add_data(line)
  1553	    }
  1554	    data_done() {
  1555	        this.pause()
  1556	        this.totalbytes += this.transaction.data_bytes
  1557	
  1558	        // Check message size limit
  1559	        if (cfg.max.bytes && this.transaction.data_bytes > cfg.max.bytes) {
  1560	            this.lognotice(`Incoming message exceeded max size of ${cfg.max.bytes}`)
  1561	            return plugins.run_hooks('max_data_exceeded', this)
  1562	        }
  1563	
  1564	        // Check max received headers count
  1565	        if (this.transaction.header.get_all('received').length > cfg.headers.max_received) {
  1566	            this.logerror('Incoming message had too many Received headers')
  1567	            this.respond(550, 'Too many received headers - possible mail loop', () => {
  1568	                this.reset_transaction()
  1569	            })
  1570	            return
  1571	        }
  1572	
  1573	        // Warn if we hit the maximum parsed header lines limit
  1574	        if (this.transaction.header_lines.length >= cfg.headers.max_lines) {
  1575	            this.logwarn(`Incoming message reached maximum parsing limit of ${cfg.headers.max_lines} header lines`)
  1576	        }
  1577	
  1578	        if (cfg.headers.clean_auth_results) {
  1579	            this.auth_results_clean() // rename old A-R headers
  1580	        }
  1581	        const ar_field = this.auth_results() // assemble new one
  1582	        if (ar_field) {
  1583	            this.transaction.add_header('Authentication-Results', ar_field)
  1584	        }
  1585	
  1586	        this.transaction.end_data(() => {
  1587	            // As this will be called asynchronously,
  1588	            // make sure we still have a transaction.
  1589	            if (!this.transaction) return
  1590	            // Record the start time of this hook as we can't take too long
  1591	            // as the client will typically hang up after 2 to 3 minutes
  1592	            // despite the RFC mandating that 10 minutes should be allowed.
  1593	            this.transaction.data_post_start = Date.now()
  1594	            plugins.run_hooks('data_post', this)
  1595	        })
  1596	    }
  1597	    data_post_respond(retval, msg) {
  1598	        if (!this.transaction) return
  1599	        this.transaction.data_post_delay = (Date.now() - this.transaction.data_post_start) / 1000
  1600	        const mid = this.transaction.header.get('Message-ID') || ''

2026-05-30 07:23:38,439 - INFO - swea-agent-haraka__Haraka-3535 - 💭 THOUGHT
Now I see the flow! The `add_header` calls happen in `data_done()` at lines 1579 and 1583, BEFORE `end_data` is called at line 1586. So:

1. Data is accumulated via `accumulate_data` -> `transaction.add_data(line)`
2. When the dot is received, `data_done()` is called
3. `auth_results_clean()` is called which calls `txn.add_header('Original-Authentication-Results', ...)`
4. `ar_field = this.auth_results()` and `txn.add_header('Authentication-Results', ar_field)`
5. THEN `txn.end_data()` is called

So `add_header` is called AFTER all data has been added but BEFORE `end_data()`. At this point:
- `header_pos > 0` (the blank line was found)
- `header.parse()` was already called
- The message stream has already received all lines including headers and body

When `add_header` adds to `header_list`, the message stream's `headers` reference sees the update. Then when `end_data` is called, the message stream finalizes.

Then when the message is piped, the constructor headers (which include the appended ones) are emitted, and the raw headers are skipped. This should work...

But wait! Let me look at the `pipe` method more carefully. There's something interesting:

```js
const doRead = () => {
    if (emitCtorHeaders) {
        for (const h of this.headers) {
            source.write(Buffer.from(h.replace(/\r?\n/g, lineEndings)))
        }
        source.write(Buffer.from(lineEndings)) // EOH marker
    }
    
    if (this.#queue.length > 0) {
        // In-memory path
        if (skipRawHeaders) {
            const skipper = new HeaderSkipper()
            ...
            for (const chunk of this.#queue) skipper.write(chunk.slice(0))
            skipper.end()
        } else {
            for (const chunk of this.#queue) source.write(chunk.slice(0))
            source.end()
        }
    }
    ...
}
```

The `for (const h of this.headers)` iterates over the headers at the time of piping. Since `this.headers` is a reference to `header_list`, any headers added before piping will be included. 

But what if the headers are added AFTER `end_data` is called but BEFORE the message is piped? That should work too, because `this.headers` is a reference.

Let me look at the actual problem from a different angle. Maybe the issue is in the `reset_headers` method or in how the header/body separation is handled.

Actually, I just noticed something in the `add_data` method:

```js
add_data(line) {
    if (typeof line === 'string') {
        // This shouldn't ever happen.
        line = Buffer.from(line, this.encoding)
    }
    // is this the end of headers line?
    if (this.header_pos === 0 && (line[0] === 0x0a || (line[0] === 0x0d && line[1] === 0x0a))) {
        this.header.parse(this.header_lines)
        this.header_pos = this.header_lines.length
        ...
    } else if (this.header_pos === 0) {
        // Build up headers
        if (this.header_lines.length < (this.cfg?.headers?.max_lines || 1000)) {
            if (line[0] === 0x2e) line = line.slice(1) // Strip leading '.'
            this.header_lines.push(line.toString(this.encoding).replace(/\r\n$/, '\n'))
        }
    } else if (this.parse_body) {
        ...
    }
    
    if (!this.discard_data) this.message_stream.add_line(line)
}
```

Notice that `this.message_stream.add_line(line)` is called for EVERY line, including headers. The message stream receives the raw lines. Then in `pipe()`, if constructor headers exist, the raw headers are skipped. So the final output has:
1. Constructor headers (from `header_list`, which includes any appended headers)
2. Blank line
3. Body (from the queue, with raw headers skipped)

This should be correct.

Let me actually write a proper test to see what happens:



🎬 ACTION
cd /testbed && cat > /tmp/repro2.js << 'EOF'
'use strict'

const config = require('haraka-config')
const Transaction = require('./transaction').Transaction

// Create a transaction
const txn = new Transaction('test-uuid', config.get('smtp.ini'))

// Simulate the flow: headers, blank line, body
txn.add_data(Buffer.from('From: test@example.com\r\n'))
txn.add_data(Buffer.from('To: recipient@example.com\r\n'))
txn.add_data(Buffer.from('Subject: Test Subject\r\n'))
txn.add_data(Buffer.from('\r\n'))  // blank line separator
txn.add_data(Buffer.from('This is the body.\r\n'))
txn.add_data(Buffer.from('Another line.\r\n'))

// Now call add_header BEFORE end_data (as done in connection.js)
txn.add_header('X-Appended-Header', 'test-value')

// End data
txn.end_data(() => {
    console.log('=== Header list: ===')
    console.log(txn.header.lines())
    console.log('')
    
    // Now get the data
    txn.message_stream.get_data((body) => {
        const bodyStr = body.toString()
        console.log('=== Full message: ===')
        console.log(bodyStr)
        console.log('=== End of message ===')
        
        // Check if the header appears in the right place
        const headerPos = bodyStr.indexOf('X-Appended-Header')
        const bodyPos = bodyStr.indexOf('This is the body')
        const blankLinePos = bodyStr.indexOf('\r\n\r\n')
        
        console.log(`Header position: ${headerPos}`)
        console.log(`Body text position: ${bodyPos}`)
        console.log(`Blank line position: ${blankLinePos}`)
        
        if (headerPos === -1) {
            console.log('BUG: Header was not included at all!')
            process.exit(1)
        } else if (headerPos > bodyPos) {
            console.log('BUG: Header appears AFTER body text!')
            process.exit(1)
        } else if (headerPos < blankLinePos) {
            console.log('OK: Header appears before blank line (in headers section)')
            process.exit(0)
        } else {
            console.log('UNEXPECTED: Header position relative to blank line is unusual')
            process.exit(1)
        }
    })
})
EOF

2026-05-30 07:24:59,492 - INFO - swea-agent-haraka__Haraka-3535 - 💭 THOUGHT
So `add_header` can be called very early, during the MAIL and RCPT commands, BEFORE any data is received. That's an important case!

When `add_header` is called before any data is received:
- `this.header_pos === 0` (no blank line found yet)
- The header is added to `header_list` via `add_end`
- `reset_headers()` is NOT called because `header_pos === 0`
- Later, when data arrives and `add_data` processes the blank line, `this.header.parse(this.header_lines)` is called
- `header.parse` re-processes the header_lines and rebuilds the header_list

Wait, let me trace this more carefully. `header.parse(this.header_lines)` does:

```js
parse(lines) {
    for (const line of lines) {
        if (/^[ \t]/.test(line)) {
            this.header_list[this.header_list.length - 1] += line
        } else {
            this.header_list.push(line)
        }
    }
    ...
}
```

But `add_end` already pushed to `header_list`. So if `add_header` is called BEFORE `parse`, the `header_list` already has the added header. Then `parse` pushes the raw headers from `header_lines` to `header_list`. So the order would be:

1. `add_header('X-Envelope-To', 'value')` -> `header_list = ['X-Envelope-To: value\n']`
2. `parse(['From: ...\n', 'To: ...\n', 'Subject: ...\n'])` -> `header_list = ['X-Envelope-To: value\n', 'From: ...\n', 'To: ...\n', 'Subject: ...\n']`

The envelope header ends up FIRST in the list, which is actually like `add_leading_header`, not `add_header` (which should append). But the PR says the issue is that headers end up at the end of the message body, not that they're in the wrong order in the header section.

Hmm, let me look at the `header.parse` function again:

```js
parse(lines) {
    for (const line of lines) {
        if (/^[ \t]/.test(line)) {
            this.header_list[this.header_list.length - 1] += line
        } else {
            this.header_list.push(line)
        }
    }
    
    for (const header of this.header_list) {
        const match = header.match(/^([^\s:]*):\s*([\s\S]*)$/)
        if (match) {
            this._add_header(match[1].toLowerCase(), match[2], 'push')
        } else {
            logger.lognotice(`Header did not look right: ${header}`)
        }
    }
    ...
}
```

Notice that `parse` doesn't clear `header_list` before adding to it! It just pushes more items. If `add_header` was called before `parse`, the header_list already has items, and `parse` adds more. But this could also be called multiple times...

Actually, look at line 135 in transaction.js:
```js
this.header.parse(this.header_lines)
```

This is called when the blank line is encountered. But `header_lines` only contains the raw headers from the email. The pre-added headers from `add_header` are NOT in `header_lines`; they were added directly to `header_list` via `header.add_end()`.

So the flow for early `add_header` calls:
1. `add_header('X-Envelope-To', 'value')` -> `header_list = ['X-Envelope-To: value\n']`, `headers = {'x-envelope-to': ['value']}`
2. Data arrives, headers accumulated in `header_lines`
3. Blank line encountered: `header.parse(this.header_lines)` 
   - First loop: `header_list = ['X-Envelope-To: value\n', 'From: ...\n', 'To: ...\n', ...]`
   - Second loop: iterates over ALL items in `header_list`, adds to `headers` dict
   - But wait, `X-Envelope-To` was already in `headers` from step 1! So it gets added again via `_add_header` with 'push', resulting in duplicates.

Actually, that's a potential issue but not the one described in the PR.

Let me think about the scenario where headers end up in the body. Maybe the issue is with the message stream's handling. Let me trace through the pipe:

1. `this.headers` = `['X-Envelope-To: value\n', 'From: ...\n', ...]` (has items)
2. `emitCtorHeaders = true`
3. `skipRawHeaders = true`
4. Constructor headers are emitted (including the added ones)
5. Blank line emitted
6. Raw headers from queue are skipped via HeaderSkipper
7. Body from queue is emitted

This should work. But what if the headers are added AFTER `end_data` has been called? That's also handled because `this.headers` is a reference.

Wait, I just noticed something about the `reset_headers` method:

```js
reset_headers() {
    const header_lines = this.header.lines()
    this.header_pos = header_lines.length
}
```

This method is called when `header_pos > 0`. It updates `header_pos` to the new length. But what does `header_pos` do? It's used in `add_data` to determine if we're still in headers or in body:

```js
if (this.header_pos === 0 && ...) {  // still in headers
} else if (this.header_pos === 0) {  // still accumulating headers
} else if (this.parse_body) {  // in body
}
```

Once `header_pos > 0`, we're in body mode. The `reset_headers` just updates the count. But it doesn't affect the message stream.

Let me look at the actual problem from the PR description more carefully. The example shows headers like `DKIM-Signature`, `X-Spam-Status` appearing AFTER the body text. These are headers that would be added by plugins like dkim, spamassassin, etc.

Let me check how the DKIM plugin adds headers
